title: Smart ways to start (or grow) your home fund in 2026
description: BUSINESS NEWS - Saving up for a deposit on a...
bodyText: [{"type":"contributor","text":"Adrian Goslett, Remax of Southern Africa","contributorName":"Adrian Goslett, Remax of Southern Africa","contributorDate":"Monday, 02 February 2026, 05:56"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"One of the simplest tactics to help grow your home fund is by setting up an automatic transfer system into a separate savings account. Ensure that this payment goes off after pay day to help remove the temptation to spend the money elsewhere."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"BUSINESS NEWS - Saving up for a deposit on a home can feel like one of the biggest money mountains to climb, especially as the cost of living keeps creeping up.","html":"BUSINESS NEWS - Saving up for a deposit on a home can feel like one of the biggest money mountains to climb, especially as the cost of living keeps creeping up."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"But the good news is, with a solid plan and a few smart, practical moves (and maybe a very determined piggy bank), 2026 could be the year more first-time buyers finally start making real progress towards owning their own place."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to Adrian Goslett, building a home fund doesn’t have to be overwhelming, you just need to start where you are, remain consistent, and utilise tools that help make saving a bit easier.","html":"According to Adrian Goslett, building a home fund doesn’t have to be overwhelming, you just need to start where you are, remain consistent, and utilise tools that help make saving a bit easier."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Start by setting a clear target and have a timeline in mind. Many people struggle to save because they don’t know what the end goal is. A practical first step is to estimate how much you’ll need for a deposit and upfront purchase costs, then break that number down into manageable monthly goals. Even the smallest contribution can add meaningful value over time if you remain consistent,” he explains.","html":"“Start by setting a clear target and have a timeline in mind. Many people struggle to save because they don’t know what the end goal is. A practical first step is to estimate how much you’ll need for a deposit and upfront purchase costs, then break that number down into manageable monthly goals. Even the smallest contribution can add meaningful value over time if you remain consistent,” he explains."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"One of the simplest tactics to help grow your home fund is by setting up an automatic transfer system into a separate savings account. Ensure that this payment goes off after pay day to help remove the temptation to spend the money elsewhere.","html":"One of the simplest tactics to help grow your home fund is by setting up an automatic transfer system into a separate savings account. Ensure that this payment goes off after pay day to help remove the temptation to spend the money elsewhere."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Choosing the right savings options also matters.","html":"Choosing the right savings options also matters."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A standard savings account may offer convenience, but it’s worth exploring higher-interest options such as fixed deposits where funds can grow faster. For those saving over a longer period, it may be beneficial to speak to a financial adviser about suitable investment options.","html":"A standard savings account may offer convenience, but it’s worth exploring higher-interest options such as fixed deposits where funds can grow faster. For those saving over a longer period, it may be beneficial to speak to a financial adviser about suitable investment options."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For those who already have a home fund in place, 2026 is a good time to reassess and grow it more strategically.","html":"For those who already have a home fund in place, 2026 is a good time to reassess and grow it more strategically."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Increasing monthly contributions by even a small percentage, redirecting annual bonuses, or making use of interest-rate changes can help the fund grow faster."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Regularly checking progress against goals can keep you motivated and ensure that your plan remains realistic.","html":"Regularly checking progress against goals can keep you motivated and ensure that your plan remains realistic."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Saving is only one part of the picture. Preparing for homeownership also means understanding the purchase process, knowing your affordability range, and putting yourself in a better financial position before you start viewing properties,” concludes Goslett.","html":"“Saving is only one part of the picture. Preparing for homeownership also means understanding the purchase process, knowing your affordability range, and putting yourself in a better financial position before you start viewing properties,” concludes Goslett."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Whether you are starting from scratch or looking to grow what you already have, small smart decisions made early in 2026 could bring you closer to owning a home than you think.","html":"Whether you are starting from scratch or looking to grow what you already have, small smart decisions made early in 2026 could bring you closer to owning a home than you think."}]

For those saving over a longer period, it may be beneficial to speak to a financial adviser about suitable investment options.","html":"A standard savings account may offer convenience, but it’s worth exploring higher-interest options such as fixed deposits where funds can grow faster. For those saving over a longer period, it may be beneficial to speak to a financial adviser about suitable investment options."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For those who already have a home fund in place, 2026 is a good time to reassess and grow it more strategically.","html":"For those who already have a home fund in place, 2026 is a good time to reassess and grow it more strategically."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Increasing monthly contributions by even a small percentage, redirecting annual bonuses, or making use of interest-rate changes can help the fund grow faster."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Regularly checking progress against goals can keep you motivated and ensure that your plan remains realistic.","html":"Regularly checking progress against goals can keep you motivated and ensure that your plan remains realistic."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Saving is only one part of the picture. Preparing for homeownership also means understanding the purchase process, knowing your affordability range, and putting yourself in a better financial position before you start viewing properties,” concludes Goslett.","html":"“Saving is only one part of the picture. Preparing for homeownership also means understanding the purchase process, knowing your affordability range, and putting yourself in a better financial position before you start viewing properties,” concludes Goslett."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Whether you are starting from scratch or looking to grow what you already have, small smart decisions made early in 2026 could bring you closer to owning a home than you think.","html":"Whether you are starting from scratch or looking to grow what you already have, small smart decisions made early in 2026 could bring you closer to owning a home than you think."}]

images.alt: Smart ways to start (or grow) your home fund in 2026

author: Adrian Goslett, Remax of Southern Africa
teaser: One of the simplest tactics to help grow your home fund is by setting up an automatic transfer system into a separate savings account. Ensure that this payment goes off after pay day to help remove the temptation to spend the money elsewhere.
contributor: Adrian Goslett, Remax of Southern Africa
featuredCaption: Photo for illustration purposes only. Image: Canva
